---
title: "Day 3 - From Models to Decisions: Bridging Science and Regional Needs"
format: html
---

::: top-banner
:::

::: text-center
*Wednesday, June 10, 2026*
:::

## Objective

Translate scientific and modeling progress into concrete next steps, aligning technical development with regional priorities and decision-making needs.

# Session 5: Working Groups - Technical Development and Regional Priorities (09.00 - 12.30)

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
This session is structured around parallel working groups, bringing together participants to focus on complementary aspects of the project and define priorities for the next phase. The breakout discussions will be facilitated by <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Fausto-Arias-Zumbado" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Fausto Arias</a> and <a href="https://example.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Dylan Glave</a>.

- **09:00 - 09:30: Introduction and framing of the working groups**

    <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Fausto-Arias-Zumbado" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Fausto Arias</a> and <a href="https://example.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Dylan Glave</a> will introduce the objectives of the working groups, explain the structure of the breakout discussions, and highlight the key questions that will guide the session.

- **09:30 - 10:45: Parallel Working Groups - Technical Development and Regional Priorities**

    Participants will split into two groups:

    1. **[Technical](../participants/technical-group.html)**  
       Focus on modeling development, climate scenarios, data integration, and defining what is realistically achievable in the next phase of the project.

    2. **[Stakeholder Engagement and Regional Priorities](../participants/nontechnical-group.html)**  
       Focus on regional needs, policy relevance, expected outputs, and how results can support decision-making across institutions.

    Each group will work through a set of guiding questions and identify key priorities, gaps, and next steps.

    At the end of the session, each group will prepare a brief synthesis of their discussion.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

:::: text-center
::: section-small
*Coffee break (10.45 - 11.00)*
:::
::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Session 6: Plenary Synthesis - Aligning Technical Capacity and Regional Needs (11.00 - 12.00)

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Each group will present a short synthesis of their discussions:

- **[Technical](../participants/technical-group.html) - "What we can do"**  
  Feasible modeling approaches, selected climate scenarios, data readiness, and key constraints.

- **[Stakeholder Engagement and Regional Priorities](../participants/nontechnical-group.html) - "What we need"**  
  Priority outputs, regional needs, and expectations from a policy and implementation perspective.

This session will focus on aligning both perspectives:

- Where do technical capabilities meet stakeholder needs?
- Where are the main gaps or mismatches?
- What adjustments are needed to move forward effectively?
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Session 7: Discussion and agreement on next steps (12.00 - 12.30)

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
This closing discussion will be used to identify the most important actions to carry forward, align priorities across teams, and clarify short-term deliverables. Participants will contribute ideas in plenary, and priorities will be consolidated and grouped into shared themes.

- **Identification of priority actions**  
  Ideas will be collected and clustered into key themes emerging from the discussion.

- **Alignment across teams and workstreams**  
  Participants will help identify where scientific, technical, and regional priorities overlap most clearly.

- **Definition of short-term deliverables**  
  The session will aim to highlight the most immediate and broadly supported next steps.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

:::: text-center
::: section-small
*Lunch break (12.30 - 14.30)*
:::
::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
- **14:30 - 17:30**: Outdoor activity *(snorkeling or local site visit)*
- **Description**: Outdoor activity designed to encourage informal exchange, strengthen connections among participants, and experience the local environment. Depending on logistics and weather conditions, this may include snorkeling or a visit to a nearby site of interest.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
- **19:00 - 21:00**: Group dinner *(location to be confirmed)*
- **Description**: Dinner outside the workshop venue to continue the day's conversations in a more relaxed setting. This will also be an opportunity to share reflections, strengthen collaborations, and celebrate the progress made during the workshop.
:::
